signal_evidence.consumer_stress_tier: RH targets wealthy and affluent consumers; no stress signals present. Management describes pent-up demand and a frozen market awaiting rate relief, not a demand destruction environment: 'There's pent up demand. And I think that's a good thing when you look at it.'
signal_evidence.capex_direction: International gallery openings (Brussels, Madrid, Newport Beach, Palo Alto, Raleigh, Montecito, Cleveland), RH Sydney development, and Waterworks showroom expansion represent accelerating platform investment: 'Our plan to expand the RH brand globally, address new markets locally, and transform our North American galleries represents a multi-billion dollar opportunity.'
signal_evidence.pricing_power: Management is actively sharpening the value equation after acknowledging prior pricing arrogance, suggesting pricing power is present but being deliberately moderated to gain share: 'I think we were arrogant about pricing... everything has to go through a lens of design quality value in that order.'
signal_evidence.management_tone_shift: Management is materially more confident than implied by the prior year's cautious posture, citing specific product launches, demand data, and a multi-quarter transformation thesis: 'We are highly confident. We think we have enough data and information to read... we believe we're going to deliver these numbers or more.'
